title: quercus dumosa
intro: Quercus dumosa, also known as the stunted evergreen oak, is a species of evergreen shrub or small tree that belongs to the beech family, Fagaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits associated with Quercus dumosa include its potential to reduce blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and protect against certain types of cancer. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tea, decoction, or infusion, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
